title: From Babylon to New Jerusalem
summary: The last movement of Revelation contrasts the counterfeit city with the city of God, ending with judgment, healing, and the face of God.

sections.title: Babylon exposed
body: Revelation's final chapters expose Babylon before they unveil the New Jerusalem. Babylon is more than one ancient city. It is the symbolic name for organized rebellion, false worship, spiritual intoxication, worldly luxury, and illicit alliance between religion and power. It appears great, wealthy, and secure, yet heaven announces its fall before the world fully sees it.

The call to come out of Babylon is therefore a call to separation from false worship and shared guilt. It is not a call to arrogance. It is a mercy call. God's people are summoned out because judgment is real and because Christ refuses to leave sincere souls trapped in confusion.

sections.title: Hallelujah and the King
body: When Babylon falls, heaven sings. The hallelujahs of Revelation 19 are not shallow celebration over suffering; they are worship because God's judgments are true and righteous. The corrupting power that deceived the nations and persecuted the saints is finally answered. The marriage supper of the Lamb then appears as the joyful counterpart to Babylon's harlot imagery.

The rider on the white horse comes as faithful and true. He judges and makes war in righteousness. The beast and false prophet do not negotiate the future; they are defeated. Revelation's final conflict is serious, but it is not evenly matched. The Lamb who was slain is also King of kings and Lord of lords.

sections.title: Millennium and final judgment
body: Revelation 20 shows the thousand years, the first resurrection, the reign of the saints, the final release of Satan, and the great white throne judgment. Satan is bound by circumstance, with no nations left to deceive during the millennial period. The redeemed participate in judgment, not because they improve God's verdict, but because God's justice is opened to them.

The final uprising after the thousand years reveals that sin has not been misunderstood; it is incurably hostile to God when left unrepented. The lake of fire is the second death, the final eradication of sin, death, and the grave. Judgment is not endless rebellion preserved forever; it is the end of rebellion so creation can be made new.

sections.title: The New Jerusalem
body: After Babylon falls and sin is judged, John sees the holy city. The New Jerusalem descends from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband. The contrast is deliberate. Babylon is a counterfeit woman-city that intoxicates the nations; New Jerusalem is the bride-city where God dwells with His people.

The final vision is not merely escape to a better place. It is the restoration of creation, covenant, worship, and life. God wipes away every tear. Death is gone. The river of life flows. The tree of life bears fruit. The servants of God see His face, His name is in their foreheads, and they reign forever and ever.

Revelation ends by forcing a choice between two cities. Babylon offers intoxication, status, and counterfeit worship, but it falls. New Jerusalem offers the presence of God, the healing of creation, and life with the Lamb.
The final invitation is therefore not only about understanding the end. It is about leaving what cannot stand and receiving the water of life freely.
